content.problemStatement:
Chef's father has bought three cakes for him, his brother, and sister. Unfortunately, one of the cakes is larger than the other two, so they need to decide who gets the larger cake. Chef's father decides to make them play a game, and the winner will get the larger cake. The game is as follows:

The father will choose an interval of numbers and give it to them. All three children will have to write an integer from the interval [both endpoints inclusive], and the child whose chosen number is the middle number when all three numbers are sorted wins the game, and hence the larger cake.

If there is a tie for the middle number, the father would choose the winner uniformly randomly from all three children. Note that even if only two children had tied for the middle element, the father will choose from all three children to pick the winner randomly.

Luckily, Chef has an advantage! He knows that both his brother and sister have a favorite digit, and he knows that both of them will choose a number from within the interval such that the number of times that their favorite digit appears in the number as a digit is maximized. If there are multiple numbers that satisfy this criterion, then they will pick any one of them uniformly at random.

For example:


If the interval is [10, 23], and the brother's favorite digit is 2, then he will choose 22, because 2 appears in it twice, and that is the maximum you can get.
If the interval is [10, 21], and the brother's favorite digit is 2, then he will choose one number from {12, 20, 21} uniformly at random.
Chef doesn't know how exactly he can utilize this information to win the larger cake. Can you help him? Given the information about the interval, and his siblings' favorite digits, find out which number he should pick, so as to maximize his chance of winning. If there are more than one number with the same maximum probability of winning, then output the smallest number among them.


Input Format

The first line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. The description of each test case follows.
Each test case contains a single line of input, with four integers: A,B,C,DA, B, C, DA,B,C,D. The interval is denoted by [A,B][A, B][A,B], the brother's favorite digit is CCC, and the sister's favorite digit is DDD.


Output Format

For each test case, output in a single line, the answer: the number which Chef should choose to maximize his chance of winning.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first test case, the brother will choose 22, and the sister will choose 33. The Chef can choose any of {23, 24, ..., 32} and win the game. But you should output the smallest among them, and hence the answer is 23.
